insight: Polar was unstable. Creem.io offers better checkout flow. Implementation requires removing duplicate proxy files.

context: Professional coach management of multiple athletes.
insight: Single-user anthropometry is insufficient for commercial clubs.
fix: Integrated athlete_name and id_number in OCR and database. Dashboard filters by ID for historical tracking.

insight: Coaches need real-time control at the pool edge without manual data entry.
fix: Design as a PWA with training plan sync, automated set timers (@1:30), and volume logging to Neon.

insight: Residual n8n proxies cause confusion and security risks if the N8N instance is down.
fix: Deleted api/n8n-proxy and api/n8n-transcribe. All business logic is now 100% native Next.js.

context: OpenRouter often renames or deprecates model IDs without clear immediate notification in all docs.
insight: Using old names like google/gemini-flash-1.5 leads to 404. Using :free suffix on high-demand models leads to 429.
fix: Always use the latest verified IDs from the API /models endpoint: google/gemini-2.5-flash, google/gemini-2.5-pro.

insight: Stateless APIs cause models to repeat instructions (like greetings) because they lack context of previous turns.
fix: Pass the full 'history' array from the client to the API and include it in the LLM prompt payload.

insight: Secondary logos (lime, red) should be avoided in favor of the official /logo.png (Yellow/Green S).
fix: Update all img src to /logo.png and ensure the file exists in public/.

context: Agent agentic loop for complex tasks.
insight: Implicit planning leads to fragile execution and circular logic.
fix: Formalized the use of <thought> blocks for explicit reasoning and planning, coupled with a mandatory memory-update phase at the end of every successful execution.
